intro: Jacquemontia sphaerostigma, also known as the round-fruited snakevine or snakevine, is a perennial vine native to tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its woody stems and small, white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
